title: Workday In The Super Bowl, Continues To Be A Buy
text: Workday is a top-tier cloud SaaS company. The business is firing on all cylinders, stock-based compensation remains a problem across the industry.

title: Workday Layoffs 2023: What to Know About the Latest WDAY Job Cuts
text: Workday (NASDAQ: WDAY ) layoffs are on the way as the business software company reveals plans to cut 3% of its global workforce. According to a statement from Workday, the layoffs are the result of a challenging economic environment.

title: 3 Tech Stocks That Will Be Big Winners in 2023
text: After a rough 2022, there's a great deal of opportunity to capitalize on tech stocks that have been overlooked. Better, investing in tech stocks is a great way to diversify your portfolio and benefit from the technology industry's growth.

title: 3 SaaS Stocks to Buy Now
text: These companies have a brighter future than it may appear.

title: Workday: Looking Attractive At Key Levels
text: Workday's revenue performance over the past few years is remarkable, and revenue for 2022 increased by 19% compared to the previous year. Despite strong revenue, Workday's share price dropped more than 50% in 2022, producing a great buying opportunity for investors.

title: U.S. Wide-Moat Stocks On Sale: The January 2023 Heat Map
text: Our 3-step process focuses on wide-moat stocks (as per Morningstar's rating). We are only interested in those targets that are attractively valued in historical comparison.

title: 5 Top Stocks Likely to Come Up With an Earnings Beat
text: These top-ranked stocks are likely to beat on the bottom line in their next release. Tap Yum China (YUMC), Chewy (CHWY), Phillips 66 (PSX), Workday (WDAY) and Sprinklr (CXM).
